top_3_stories.headline: Sensex and Nifty extend gains, close higher on Thursday
top_3_stories.what_happened: Indian benchmarks ended firmly higher on July 2, 2026. The Nifty 50 closed at 24,175.70, up about 169.85 points (+0.71%), and the BSE Sensex closed at 77,502.12, up about 579.48 points (+0.75%) versus the previous session, extending the market's recovery.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: A second straight up-day was led by a broad rebound, with technology stocks swinging from the prior session's biggest drag to the day's biggest support, showing how quickly sector leadership can rotate.
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: Index levels and daily point moves are backward-looking data, not signals; educational value is in seeing how sector rotation drives a benchmark's daily move.

top_3_stories.headline: Nifty IT jumps ~3%, leads rebound after four-day slide
top_3_stories.what_happened: The Nifty IT index surged more than 3% on July 2 after four consecutive sessions of losses. Infosys rose about 3.9%, HCLTech about 3.4%, TCS about 2.6% and Tech Mahindra about 2.3%, with several IT names among the top Nifty gainers. The bounce followed the prior day's slump triggered by a KPIT Technologies revenue warning.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: The one-day swing from worst-performing to best-performing sector illustrates mean-reversion / bargain-buying dynamics after a sharp correction, and how single-stock news (a KPIT warning) can move a whole index for a day before sentiment resets.
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: A single day's sector bounce reflects short-term positioning, not a change in fundamentals; understanding why an index moved matters more than the move itself.

top_3_stories.headline: Auto stocks in focus on strong June sales numbers
top_3_stories.what_happened: The Nifty Auto index gained around 1% as automakers reported robust June 2026 dispatches. Mahindra & Mahindra rose after reporting an about 37% year-on-year jump in total June vehicle sales, while Maruti Suzuki and Tata Motors also advanced and Ashok Leyland gained on healthy commercial-vehicle dispatches.
top_3_stories.why_it_matters: Monthly wholesale dispatch figures are a widely watched, fundamentals-linked data point for the auto sector, showing how real business updates (unit sales) feed into how a sector trades that day.
top_3_stories.investor_takeaway: Monthly sales are one input among many; a strong month is company disclosure to study, not a recommendation to act.
key_numbers: M&M June total vehicle sales: about +37% YoY
Maruti Suzuki and Tata Motors: advanced ~2%

_meta.reuse_note: same-day verified research; sources, figures and timestamps unchanged
